description_long: This object is a pale, elongated structure with a gently curved profile and a series of evenly spaced, tooth-like projections along one edge. The surface appears hard and slightly textured, with a matte finish and subtle color variations ranging from off-white to light beige. The object has a prominent vertical extension at one end, resembling a ramus, and a thinner, tapering section at the opposite end. There are no visible windows, engines, wings, or other features typically associated with an airplane. The overall form is reminiscent of a lower jawbone, with a smooth, continuous contour and no apparent separable parts. The object lacks any markings, insignia, or mechanical components.
description: A pale, curved structure with tooth-like projections along one edge and a prominent vertical extension at one end. The surface is hard, matte, and off-white to beige in color.
description_view_0: This view shows the object in profile, highlighting the curved body, the row of tooth-like projections, and the prominent vertical extension at one end.
description_view_1: From this edge-on view, the object appears thin and elongated, with the tooth-like projections forming a visible ridge along one side.
description_view_2: This view mirrors the profile of view 0 but from the opposite side, again showing the curved body and the series of projections.
description_view_3: Looking at the object from the end, the vertical extension and the curvature of the body are most prominent, with the projections partially visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: No wings or wingspan configuration visible.
